Description: AI based robot for mapping, navigation and Computer Vision Applications
Sensors: IMU, Stereo Camera, LiDAR, Wheel Encoders
Educational Applications: Embedded AI Labs: Students deploy models on the Raspberry Pi 5 to perform stereo-vision distance estimation and object tracking.
Research Applications: Testing multi-sensor fusion for real-time 3D depth estimation and edge based semantic mapping

Description: LiDAR enabled Mobile robot for mapping and navigation
Educational Applications: ROS 2 Navigation Course: Students use LiDAR data to program 2D costmaps, obstacle avoidance, and path-planning algorithms.
Research Applications: Developing autonomous frontier-exploration algorithms to optimize unknown enviornment mapping without human inputs

Description: AI based robot for mapping, navigation and Computer Vision Applications
Sensors: IMU, LiDAR, Wheel Encoders, stereo camera
Educational Applications: Autonomous Driving Thesis: Graduate students write CUDA-accelerated code on the Jetson Xavier NX for high-speed lane tracking and sign detection.
Research Applications: Writing GPU accelarated deep reinforcement learning models for high speed dynamic obstacle avoidance

Description: LiDAR enabled drone with Long Range communication for basic flight planning
Educational Applications: Flight Dynamics Labs: Aerospace students modify Pixhawk parameters to analyze PID loop stability and aerodynamic disturbance rejection.
Research Applications: Researching LiDAR based indoor odometry and adaptive PID flight controllers to handle severe wind disturbances

Description: A Complete Kit for STEM Education
Sensors: IMU, Wheel Encoders, Humidity, weight sensor
Educational Applications: K-12 Physical Computing: Middle/high school students write MicroPython scripts to map humidity and load weight in classroom science projects.
Research Applications: Deploying highly quantized neural networks for anomaly detection

Description: Swarm Intelligent platform for connectivity of multiple robots and swarm behavior
Educational Applications: Swarm Intelligence Seminars: Computer science students program decentralized Wi-Fi/BLE mesh networks to orchestrate multi-robot formation control.
Research Applications: Implementing mesh network protocols and consensus algorithms for multi-agent formation control and emergent behavior.
